\section*{Glossary}
\addcontentsline{toc}{section}{Glossary}
\label{sec:glossary}

\begin{description}
\item[\hypertarget{glossary:activation-patching}{Activation patching}]
\hyperref[sec:scheming]{\textcolor{glscolor}{$\to$}} the canonical causal-intervention method

\item[\hypertarget{glossary:activation-steering}{Activation steering}]
\hyperref[sec:sycophancy]{\textcolor{glscolor}{$\to$}} Inference-time intervention that adds $\alpha \hat{\mathbf{d}}$ to a residual-stream activation to amplify (or, with $\alpha < 0$, suppress) a probed direction. The Representation Engineering / Vennemeyer 2025 line `[vennemeyer2025-sycophancy-not-one-thing FORUM-SIGNAL]`.

\item[\hypertarget{glossary:agentic-benchmark}{Agentic benchmark}]
\hyperref[sec:reasoning-benchmarks]{\textcolor{glscolor}{$\to$}} Evaluation in which a model autonomously plans, takes actions in an environment, observes results, and replans, scored by an end-state verifier rather than a turn-by-turn rubric. Distinct from question-answering eval. Canonical examples: SWE-bench, GAIA, OSWorld, tau-bench `[jimenez2024-swebench, mialon2023-gaia, xie2024-osworld, yao2024-tau-bench]`.

\item[\hypertarget{glossary:alignment-faking}{Alignment faking}]
\hyperref[sec:alignment-faking]{\textcolor{glscolor}{$\to$}} Operational sub-form of deceptive alignment:

\item[\hypertarget{glossary:answer-letter-prior-2}{Answer-letter prior}]
\hyperref[sec:knowledge-benchmarks]{\textcolor{glscolor}{$\to$}} The training-data-induced bias that pre-trained LMs do not have uniform priors over A/B/C/D in MCQ items. Letter bias can favor one option by several percentage points; 10-way MCQ (MMLU-Pro) dilutes this effect `[wang2024-mmlu-pro §sec-delta]`.

\item[\hypertarget{glossary:asl}{ASL} (AI Safety Level)]
\hyperref[sec:dangerous-capability]{\textcolor{glscolor}{$\to$}} Anthropic's Responsible Scaling Policy tier framework. ASL-1 (low risk, no autonomy) $\to$ ASL-2 (current frontier baseline) $\to$ ASL-3 (substantially elevated risk; specific deployment controls required) $\to$ ASL-4 (autonomous AI risk; not yet deployed). Each tier specifies capability-evaluation triggers and security/containment commitments `[anthropic-rsp-2024]`.

\item[\hypertarget{glossary:asr}{ASR} (Attack Success Rate)]
\hyperref[sec:introduction]{\textcolor{glscolor}{$\to$}} Headline metric in adversarial-robustness benchmarks: fraction of (behavior, attack) pairs for which the model produces an output the judge rates harmful. Not directly comparable across papers unless the judge, behaviors, and attack family match `[chao2024-jailbreakbench §abstract]`.

\item[\hypertarget{glossary:attack-success-rate}{Attack success rate} (ASR)]
\hyperref[sec:measuring-framing]{\textcolor{glscolor}{$\to$}} The fraction of adversarial prompts

\item[\hypertarget{glossary:auxiliary-confidence-loss}{Auxiliary confidence loss}]
\hyperref[sec:scalable-oversight]{\textcolor{glscolor}{$\to$}} W2S training-side intervention that

\item[\hypertarget{glossary:calibration-set}{Calibration set}]
\hyperref[sec:adversarial-robustness]{\textcolor{glscolor}{$\to$}} A small set of representative input sequences (typically 128 sequences $\times$ 2048 tokens) run through the network to collect activation statistics that drive quantization decisions. \citep[\S 2; xiao2023-smoothquant §3.2]{frantar2022}

\item[\hypertarget{glossary:capture-the-flag}{Capture-the-Flag} (CTF)]
\hyperref[sec:dangerous-capability]{\textcolor{glscolor}{$\to$}} Cybersecurity task format adopted by Cybench: agents must exploit a target system to retrieve a flag string (typically `flag{...}`), with success scored by string match. [cybench2024]

\item[\hypertarget{glossary:chain-of-thought}{Chain-of-thought} (CoT)]
\hyperref[sec:measuring-framing]{\textcolor{glscolor}{$\to$}} A prompting and decoding pattern in which the model produces an intermediate sequence of reasoning steps $z$ before emitting a final answer $y$. In few-shot CoT (Wei 2022), the prompt contains exemplars with hand-written reasoning traces; in zero-shot CoT (Kojima 2022), the trigger phrase "Let's think step by step" elicits the same behaviour without exemplars. \citep[\S 1; kojima2022 §3]{wei2022-cot}

\item[\hypertarget{glossary:circuit}{Circuit}]
\hyperref[sec:introduction]{\textcolor{glscolor}{$\to$}} a sub-graph $C$ of a model's computational graph $M$

\item[\hypertarget{glossary:claude}{Claude}]
\hyperref[sec:agentic-benchmarks]{\textcolor{glscolor}{$\to$}} Anthropic's proprietary decoder-only LLM family. No public architecture paper; structural family shares the decoder-only Transformer lineage.

\item[\hypertarget{glossary:constitution}{Constitution}]
\hyperref[sec:sycophancy]{\textcolor{glscolor}{$\to$}} A list of principles ("the assistant should not be racist," etc.) used by the feedback model to evaluate / critique responses in CAI. Typical size: ~16 principles `[bai2022-cai §3]`.

\item[\hypertarget{glossary:constitutional-ai}{Constitutional AI} (CAI)]
\hyperref[sec:scheming]{\textcolor{glscolor}{$\to$}} Anthropic 2022 framework for training harmless assistants without human harm labels. Two stages: SL-CAI (sample $\to$ critique under principle $\to$ revise $\to$ SFT) and RL-CAI (sample pairs $\to$ AI judge under principle $\to$ train RM $\to$ PPO) `[bai2022-cai §3-4]`.

\item[\hypertarget{glossary:consultancy}{Consultancy}]
\hyperref[sec:scalable-oversight]{\textcolor{glscolor}{$\to$}} Single-debater variant of debate: one AI agent

\item[\hypertarget{glossary:crescendomation}{Crescendomation}]
\hyperref[sec:adversarial-robustness]{\textcolor{glscolor}{$\to$}} The automated implementation of Crescendo: an attacker LLM generates the escalation chain. The 29-71% ASR uplift number refers to Crescendomation specifically `[russinovich2024-crescendo §sec-asr-uplift]`.

\item[\hypertarget{glossary:dangerous-capability-evaluation-2}{Dangerous-capability evaluation}]
\hyperref[sec:agentic-benchmarks]{\textcolor{glscolor}{$\to$}} Pre-deployment eval that tests whether a model can autonomously carry out a task whose successful completion would constitute a threat (cyber CTFs, autonomous replication, biology / CBRN tasks). Inverts the usual "model refuses harm" frame to "model could *do* harm if it tried" `[metr-autonomy-2024; cybench2024 FORUM-SIGNAL]`.

\item[\hypertarget{glossary:data-contamination}{Data contamination}]
\hyperref[sec:contamination]{\textcolor{glscolor}{$\to$}} The presence of benchmark items, gold answers, or close paraphrases in a model's training corpus, biasing the benchmark score upward. Taxonomy: input contamination, label contamination, distribution contamination, indirect contamination, generative contamination. \citep[\S abstract]{contamination-survey-2025}

\item[\hypertarget{glossary:debate}{Debate} (AI safety)]
\hyperref[sec:introduction]{\textcolor{glscolor}{$\to$}} Two AI debaters in a zero-sum game judged

\item[\hypertarget{glossary:deceptive-alignment}{Deceptive alignment}]
\hyperref[sec:alignment-faking]{\textcolor{glscolor}{$\to$}} Hubinger et al. 2019 theoretical concept:

\item[\hypertarget{glossary:decontamination}{Decontamination} (Phi-4 sense)]
\hyperref[sec:contamination]{\textcolor{glscolor}{$\to$}} Held-out test sets, especially November 2024 AMC math contests and unleaked HumanEval+, used to verify the synthetic data did not leak benchmark answers via the teacher. \citep[\S 1.1]{phi4}

\item[\hypertarget{glossary:dpo}{DPO} (Direct Preference Optimization)]
\hyperref[sec:sycophancy]{\textcolor{glscolor}{$\to$}} Closed-form, classification-loss reformulation of RLHF: trains the policy directly on preference pairs by inverting the closed-form RL optimum. No reward model, no on-policy sampling, no PPO loop `[rafailov2023-dpo §4 Eq.7]`.

\item[\hypertarget{glossary:end-state-verifier}{End-state verifier}]
\hyperref[sec:agentic-benchmarks]{\textcolor{glscolor}{$\to$}} A scoring function $V(s_T, g)$ that checks the post-trajectory environment state $s_T$ against a goal $g$, accepting any path that reaches the correct end-state. Contrast: turn-by-turn rubric (rewards specific actions). \citep[\S sec-task-spec]{xie2024-osworld}

\item[\hypertarget{glossary:feature}{Feature}]
\hyperref[sec:watermarking]{\textcolor{glscolor}{$\to$}} a direction $\mathbf{d} \in \mathbb{R}^n$ in some

\item[\hypertarget{glossary:frontiermath}{FrontierMath}]
\hyperref[sec:introduction]{\textcolor{glscolor}{$\to$}} Epoch AI's benchmark of research-grade math problems authored and cross-vetted by 60+ working mathematicians, with single canonical numerical/symbolic answers parseable by automated checker. <2% solve rate at Nov 2024 launch. \citep[\S abstract]{glazer2024-frontiermath}

\item[\hypertarget{glossary:goal-guarding}{Goal-guarding}]
\hyperref[sec:scheming]{\textcolor{glscolor}{$\to$}} One of the Apollo six-probe scheming behaviors:

\item[\hypertarget{glossary:google-proof-construction}{Google-proof construction}]
\hyperref[sec:knowledge-benchmarks]{\textcolor{glscolor}{$\to$}} Question design protocol where non-expert validators with 30+ minutes of web access score only marginally above random guessing (e.g., 34% on GPQA's 4-way MCQ vs. 25% baseline). The operationalization of "specialized expertise required." \citep[\S abstract]{rein2023-gpqa}

\item[\hypertarget{glossary:gpqa-diamond-2}{GPQA Diamond}]
\hyperref[sec:knowledge-benchmarks]{\textcolor{glscolor}{$\to$}} 198-question subset of GPQA (Rein et al. 2023) where two PhD experts agree on the answer and at least one non-expert validator with web access got it wrong. The "Google-proof" property is operationalized via the 34% non-expert-with-web baseline. Saturated at frontier as of 2026 (>90%) `[rein2023-gpqa §abstract]`.

\item[\hypertarget{glossary:grpo-2}{GRPO} (Group Relative Policy Optimisation)]
\hyperref[sec:reasoning-benchmarks]{\textcolor{glscolor}{$\to$}} Critic-free PPO variant (Shao 2024). Per-prompt group of $G$ samples; advantage is each sample's reward minus group mean, divided by group std. Eliminates the value-network requirement. \citep[\S 4.1; deepseek-r1 §2.1]{shao2024}

\item[\hypertarget{glossary:gui-grounding}{GUI grounding}]
\hyperref[sec:agentic-benchmarks]{\textcolor{glscolor}{$\to$}} The ability to map a natural-language target ("click the Save button") to the correct pixel coordinates on a screen. Identified as the dominant failure mode for vision-language agents on OSWorld at launch. \citep[\S sec-results]{xie2024-osworld}

\item[\hypertarget{glossary:harmbench-2}{HarmBench}]
\hyperref[sec:introduction]{\textcolor{glscolor}{$\to$}} Mazeika et al. 2024 standardized red-team evaluation framework: 510 behaviors in four functional categories, 18 attack methods, 33 target LLMs/defenses. Used by AISI for pre-deployment evals `[harmbench2024 §abstract]`.

\item[\hypertarget{glossary:harmbench-judge}{HarmBench-Judge}]
\hyperref[sec:adversarial-robustness]{\textcolor{glscolor}{$\to$}} The fine-tuned classifier (Llama-2-13B family, fine-tuned on labeled harmful/non-harmful generations) shipped with HarmBench as the standardized judge for the 510 behaviors `[harmbench2024 §sec-structure]`.

\item[\hypertarget{glossary:helm}{HELM} (Holistic Evaluation of Language Models)]
\hyperref[sec:introduction]{\textcolor{glscolor}{$\to$}} Stanford CRFM 2022 framework that scores models on 7 metric axes (accuracy, calibration, robustness, fairness, bias, toxicity, efficiency) across 16+ scenarios. Structural argument: benchmarks should ship metric bundles, not single metrics. \citep[\S sec-metrics]{liang2022-helm}

\item[\hypertarget{glossary:hle}{HLE} (Humanity's Last Exam)]
\hyperref[sec:introduction]{\textcolor{glscolor}{$\to$}} 2,500-question multimodal cross-domain frontier benchmark from Scale AI / CAIS. Designed as the post-MMLU broad-domain frontier target. \citep[\S abstract]{phan2025-hle}

\item[\hypertarget{glossary:in-context-scheming}{In-context scheming}]
\hyperref[sec:introduction]{\textcolor{glscolor}{$\to$}} Apollo's experimental subset of scheming:

\item[\hypertarget{glossary:iterated-amplification}{Iterated amplification} (IDA)]
\hyperref[sec:introduction]{\textcolor{glscolor}{$\to$}} Christiano 2018: recursive

\item[\hypertarget{glossary:jailbreakbench-2}{JailbreakBench}]
\hyperref[sec:introduction]{\textcolor{glscolor}{$\to$}} Chao et al. 2024 reproducibility-focused jailbreak benchmark: 100 OpenAI-policy-aligned behaviors, evolving repository of jailbreak artifacts (the actual successful prompts), standardized LLM-as-judge, public leaderboard. NeurIPS 2024 D\&B `[chao2024-jailbreakbench §abstract]`.

\item[\hypertarget{glossary:key}{Key} (K)]
\hyperref[sec:introduction]{\textcolor{glscolor}{$\to$}} Projected representation of positions being "queried" against. The Q$\cdot$K dot product determines attention weights.

\item[\hypertarget{glossary:llm}{LLM}]
\hyperref[sec:introduction]{\textcolor{glscolor}{$\to$}} Large Language Model. A neural network with billions of parameters trained on large text corpora to predict and generate language.

\item[\hypertarget{glossary:llm-as-judge}{LLM-as-judge}]
\hyperref[sec:knowledge-benchmarks]{\textcolor{glscolor}{$\to$}} Evaluation pattern where a strong LLM scores another model's responses (e.g., AlpacaEval, MT-Bench, Arena-Hard). Introduces a contamination axis where the same model class scores its own outputs generously. Sidestepped by string-match (GAIA, FrontierMath) or executable verifiers (SWE-bench, OSWorld).

\item[\hypertarget{glossary:lm-evaluation-harness}{lm-evaluation-harness}]
\hyperref[sec:measuring-framing]{\textcolor{glscolor}{$\to$}} EleutherAI's open-source eval infrastructure backing HF Open LLM Leaderboard. Tasks are YAML configs reduced to three primitives (`loglikelihood`, `loglikelihood\_rolling`, `generate\_until`). De-facto standard for open-model evaluation. [lm-eval-harness]

\item[\hypertarget{glossary:loglikelihood-scoring}{loglikelihood scoring}]
\hyperref[sec:measuring-framing]{\textcolor{glscolor}{$\to$}} MCQ evaluation method that computes $\log P(\text{option})$ for each option and picks the argmax. Default for MMLU in lm-evaluation-harness; not available for closed APIs that don't expose logprobs (which must use `generate\_until`). \citep[\S sec-request-types]{lm-eval-harness}

\item[\hypertarget{glossary:matharena}{MathArena}]
\hyperref[sec:contamination]{\textcolor{glscolor}{$\to$}} Dynamic math benchmark using post-cutoff competition problems (CMIMC, IMO, etc.) immediately upon release. Contamination-immune by construction. [matharena-2025]

\item[\hypertarget{glossary:mesa-optimizer}{Mesa-optimizer}]
\hyperref[sec:scheming]{\textcolor{glscolor}{$\to$}} Hubinger et al. 2019 concept: a learned

\item[\hypertarget{glossary:mmlu}{MMLU} (Massive Multitask Language Understanding)]
\hyperref[sec:introduction]{\textcolor{glscolor}{$\to$}} 57-subject 4-way MCQ benchmark introduced by Hendrycks et al. 2020 / ICLR 2021. 15,908 questions sourced from public exam banks (AP, GRE, MCAT, professional licensing). Foundational for the post-2021 LLM eval era; saturated at >90% on frontier models as of 2026 `[hendrycks2021-mmlu §abstract]`.

\item[\hypertarget{glossary:mmlu-pro}{MMLU-Pro}]
\hyperref[sec:measuring-framing]{\textcolor{glscolor}{$\to$}} 10-way MCQ, reasoning-augmented successor to MMLU. 12,032 items, 16-33 percentage point drop in headline score relative to MMLU at launch, 2$\times$ lower prompt-format sensitivity `[wang2024-mmlu-pro §abstract]`.

\item[\hypertarget{glossary:mmlu-redux-2}{MMLU-Redux}]
\hyperref[sec:introduction]{\textcolor{glscolor}{$\to$}} Polo et al. 2024 audit of MMLU finding 6.49% baseline error rate, with the Virology subset reaching 57% errors. Per-question corrections published; methodology audit, not a new benchmark `[mmlu-redux-2024 §abstract]`.

\item[\hypertarget{glossary:oversight-subversion}{Oversight subversion}]
\hyperref[sec:scheming]{\textcolor{glscolor}{$\to$}} Apollo six-probe scheming behavior:

\item[\hypertarget{glossary:parameters}{Parameters}]
\hyperref[sec:measuring-framing]{\textcolor{glscolor}{$\to$}} The learned weights of the model (embedding matrices, projection matrices, normalization scales, etc.).

\item[\hypertarget{glossary:performance-gap-recovered}{Performance Gap Recovered} (PGR)]
\hyperref[sec:scalable-oversight]{\textcolor{glscolor}{$\to$}} Burns et al. 2023's headline

\item[\hypertarget{glossary:perplexity}{Perplexity}]
\hyperref[sec:contamination]{\textcolor{glscolor}{$\to$}} $2^{\mathcal{L}}$ (or $e^{\mathcal{L}}$ with natural log). Measures how "surprised" the model is by the data. Lower is better.

\item[\hypertarget{glossary:probe-2}{Probe}]
\hyperref[sec:introduction]{\textcolor{glscolor}{$\to$}} A (typically linear) classifier $g: \mathbb{R}^d \to \mathcal{Y}$ trained on frozen LM activations $\mathbf{h}^{(\ell, t)}$ to predict an external property $y$. The model's parameters are held fixed; only the probe is trained. Foundational to interpretability methodology `[alain-bengio-2017 §1]`.

\item[\hypertarget{glossary:process-reward-model}{Process Reward Model} (PRM)]
\hyperref[sec:scalable-oversight]{\textcolor{glscolor}{$\to$}} A scoring function $R^P_\phi: (x, s_{1:t}) \to [0, 1]$ trained on per-step correctness labels. Dense signal — one label per step. Outperforms ORM at matched best-of-$N$ on MATH. \citep[\S 2.2, §4]{lightman2023-prm800k}

\item[\hypertarget{glossary:query}{Query} (Q)]
\hyperref[sec:alignment-faking]{\textcolor{glscolor}{$\to$}} Projected representation of the position that is "asking" for information in attention.

\item[\hypertarget{glossary:r1-zero}{R1-Zero}]
\hyperref[sec:contamination]{\textcolor{glscolor}{$\to$}} DeepSeek-R1-Zero, the version of R1 trained with **no SFT cold-start** — pure GRPO RL applied directly to the DeepSeek-V3 base. Achieves AIME 2024 pass@1 of 77.9% `[deepseek-r1 §2.3]`.

\item[\hypertarget{glossary:random-guess-ceiling}{Random-guess ceiling}]
\hyperref[sec:knowledge-benchmarks]{\textcolor{glscolor}{$\to$}} The score a uniform-random model would achieve. 25% for 4-way MCQ (MMLU, GPQA), 10% for 10-way MCQ (MMLU-Pro), ~0% for free-form numeric (FrontierMath). Lower ceiling = larger usable dynamic range above noise floor. [wang2024-mmlu-pro]

\item[\hypertarget{glossary:reasoning-model}{Reasoning model}]
\hyperref[sec:reasoning-benchmarks]{\textcolor{glscolor}{$\to$}} An LLM trained (typically with RL on verifiable rewards) to produce long-form chain-of-thought before its final answer. o1, DeepSeek-R1, Qwen3-thinking-mode, Gemini 2.5-thinking-mode are exemplars `[deepseek-r1; kb/index/papers.json\#deepseek-r1]`.

\item[\hypertarget{glossary:red-teaming}{Red teaming} (LLM)]
\hyperref[sec:adversarial-robustness]{\textcolor{glscolor}{$\to$}} Systematic adversarial testing of an LLM

\item[\hypertarget{glossary:residual-stream}{Residual stream}]
\hyperref[sec:sycophancy]{\textcolor{glscolor}{$\to$}} the linear data bus running through a

\item[\hypertarget{glossary:responsible-scaling-policy}{Responsible Scaling Policy} (RSP)]
\hyperref[sec:dangerous-capability]{\textcolor{glscolor}{$\to$}} Anthropic's pre-deployment

\item[\hypertarget{glossary:reward-hacking}{Reward hacking}]
\hyperref[sec:sycophancy]{\textcolor{glscolor}{$\to$}} Policy exploits an artifact of the reward signal that does not correspond to true quality (e.g., verbosity, sycophancy, format-shortcuts). Distinct from overoptimization in that hacking can occur even at low KL `[deepseek-r1 §2.2]`.

\item[\hypertarget{glossary:reward-model}{reward model} (RM, $r_\phi$)]
\hyperref[sec:sycophancy]{\textcolor{glscolor}{$\to$}} A neural network trained on Bradley–Terry preferences to score response quality. Initialized from $\pi^{\mathrm{SFT}}$ with a scalar head replacing the LM head. Used as the reward signal for PPO `[ouyang2022-instructgpt §3.5]`.

\item[\hypertarget{glossary:rlaif-3}{RLAIF} (Reinforcement Learning from AI Feedback)]
\hyperref[sec:sycophancy]{\textcolor{glscolor}{$\to$}} RLHF pipeline

\item[\hypertarget{glossary:rlhf}{RLHF} (Reinforcement Learning from Human Feedback)]
\hyperref[sec:introduction]{\textcolor{glscolor}{$\to$}} The 3-stage post-training pipeline: SFT $\to$ reward-model training on pairwise preferences $\to$ PPO with KL anchor against $\pi^{\mathrm{SFT}}$. Canonical reference is InstructGPT `[ouyang2022-instructgpt §3]`.

\item[\hypertarget{glossary:rlvr-3}{RLVR} (RL with Verifiable Rewards)]
\hyperref[sec:introduction]{\textcolor{glscolor}{$\to$}} RL post-training where the reward is computed mechanically from the emitted answer (regex match for math, unit-test execution for code, proof-checker for formal proofs), not from a learned reward model. The defining feature of post-2024 reasoning training. \citep[\S 2.2.1]{deepseek-r1}

\item[\hypertarget{glossary:safety-capability-parity}{Safety-capability parity}]
\hyperref[sec:adversarial-robustness]{\textcolor{glscolor}{$\to$}} Wei et al. 2023's policy recommendation: safety mechanisms must be as sophisticated as the model's full capability range. Partial-domain safety training will be defeated by attacks that target the capability/safety gap `[wei2023-jailbroken §abstract]`.

\item[\hypertarget{glossary:sandbagging}{Sandbagging}]
\hyperref[sec:dangerous-capability]{\textcolor{glscolor}{$\to$}} Strategic underperformance on a capability

\item[\hypertarget{glossary:saturation}{Saturation}]
\hyperref[sec:introduction]{\textcolor{glscolor}{$\to$}} A benchmark is saturated when frontier models score within the verifier-error-rate noise floor of one another, losing discriminative power. MMLU at >90% is saturated (verifier error rate ~6.5%); GPQA Diamond at >90% is saturated at the frontier as of 2026. [mmlu-redux-2024]

\item[\hypertarget{glossary:scalable-oversight}{Scalable oversight}]
\hyperref[sec:introduction]{\textcolor{glscolor}{$\to$}} The family of training and evaluation

\item[\hypertarget{glossary:scheming}{Scheming}]
\hyperref[sec:introduction]{\textcolor{glscolor}{$\to$}} A model schemes when, instructed to pursue a goal $G$,

\item[\hypertarget{glossary:self-consistency-2}{Self-consistency}]
\hyperref[sec:contamination]{\textcolor{glscolor}{$\to$}} Sample $N$ chain-of-thoughts independently at $T > 0$; majority-vote the final answers. Wang et al. 2022. Cheap baseline that requires no verifier.

\item[\hypertarget{glossary:self-exfiltration}{Self-exfiltration}]
\hyperref[sec:scheming]{\textcolor{glscolor}{$\to$}} Apollo six-probe scheming behavior: copying

\item[\hypertarget{glossary:situational-awareness}{Situational awareness} (LLM)]
\hyperref[sec:contamination]{\textcolor{glscolor}{$\to$}} A model's capacity to reason

\item[\hypertarget{glossary:softmax}{Softmax}]
\hyperref[sec:watermarking]{\textcolor{glscolor}{$\to$}} Converts logits to a probability distribution: $P(j) = e^{z_j} / \sum_k e^{z_k}$.

\item[\hypertarget{glossary:sparse-autoencoder}{Sparse autoencoder} (SAE)]
\hyperref[sec:scheming]{\textcolor{glscolor}{$\to$}} a wide ($M \gg n$) two-layer

\item[\hypertarget{glossary:swe-bench-verified}{SWE-Bench Verified}]
\hyperref[sec:agentic-benchmarks]{\textcolor{glscolor}{$\to$}} OpenAI's August-2024 500-task subset of SWE-bench, filtered by professional software engineers for unambiguous issue specs and fair test sets. The de-facto leaderboard target as of 2025. [jimenez2024-swebench]

\item[\hypertarget{glossary:sycophancy}{Sycophancy}]
\hyperref[sec:introduction]{\textcolor{glscolor}{$\to$}} A response is sycophantic if it is more aligned with

\item[\hypertarget{glossary:sycophancyeval}{SycophancyEval}]
\hyperref[sec:sycophancy]{\textcolor{glscolor}{$\to$}} Sharma et al. 2023's four-task free-form

\item[\hypertarget{glossary:synthid-text}{SynthID-Text}]
\hyperref[sec:watermarking]{\textcolor{glscolor}{$\to$}} Google DeepMind's tournament-sampling watermark.

\item[\hypertarget{glossary:task-horizon-metric}{Task-horizon metric} (METR)]
\hyperref[sec:agentic-benchmarks]{\textcolor{glscolor}{$\to$}} Capability-evaluation metric framing model performance as the human-time-to-complete distribution at which the model succeeds at >50%. Becoming the field's lingua franca for capability quantification across labs and external evaluators `[metr-autonomy-2024]`.

\item[\hypertarget{glossary:transformer}{Transformer}]
\hyperref[sec:scheming]{\textcolor{glscolor}{$\to$}} Neural network architecture introduced by Vaswani et al. (2017) that replaces recurrence with self-attention, allowing parallel processing of sequences.

\item[\hypertarget{glossary:value}{Value} (V)]
\hyperref[sec:reasoning-benchmarks]{\textcolor{glscolor}{$\to$}} Projected representation of the content to be aggregated. Weighted by attention scores to produce the output.

\item[\hypertarget{glossary:vocabulary}{Vocabulary} (V)]
\hyperref[sec:introduction]{\textcolor{glscolor}{$\to$}} The fixed set of all tokens a model can recognize. Size ranges from ~32K (LLaMA) to ~50K (GPT-3).

\item[\hypertarget{glossary:watermark}{Watermark} (LLM text)]
\hyperref[sec:introduction]{\textcolor{glscolor}{$\to$}} A statistical signal embedded into a
\end{description}
